title: ed discussion
markdown:
## Ed Discussion — Quick Start Guide

> **Ed Discussion** centralizes class Q&A and saves time. Supports equations, runnable code, and more.

---

## Interface

Ed Discussion features a **clean and intuitive** interface with core navigation elements:

- **Start a new thread** — opens the post composer
- **Toggle between courses** — switch context across your enrolled courses
- **Toggle between categories** — filter threads by topic or category
- **This Week** view — see recent activity at a glance

---

## Reading & Responding

- **Open a thread** to read its full content
- **Respond** directly within the thread to continue the discussion

---

## Posting a Question

To ask a question:

1. **Select Type** — choose the post type (e.g., Question, Post, Announcement)
2. **Insert Title** — give your thread a clear, descriptive title
3. **Select Category** — assign the appropriate category
4. **Compose your content** using the all-in-one editor
5. **Submit your post**

---

## Editor Features

> The editor is a **superb all-in-one** tool designed to help you express and communicate your ideas effectively.

Supported content types:

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| **Format text** | Bold, italic, headings, and other rich-text formatting |
| **Hyperlink** | Insert clickable links |
| **Create a list** | Bulleted and numbered lists |
| **Upload an image** | Attach images directly |
| **Embed a video** | Embed video content inline |
| **Upload documents** | Attach files to your post |
| **Write an equation** | LaTeX-style math rendering |
| **Write code** | Syntax-highlighted, runnable code blocks |
| **Insert web snippets** | Embed external web content |
| **Annotate images** | Mark up images with annotations |

---

## Tips and Tricks

- **Search** for relevant threads before posting to avoid duplicates
- **Stay notified** — subscribe to threads to receive updates on new replies
